name: Popeye's Fried Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first nine ingredients in a large bowl.
2. Place the crushed cornflakes in a separate bowl.
3. Mix eggs and cold water in another bowl.
4. Heat enough corn oil in a heavy, roomy skillet to fill it 1 inch deep. Make sure the oil is hot.
5. Grease a 9x12x2 baking pan.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
7. Dip each chicken piece one at a time:
- First, into the dry coating mix.
- Second, into the egg and water mixture.
- Third, into the crushed cornflakes.
- Fourth, quickly but briefly back into the dry mix.
8. Drop the coated pieces into the hot oil, skin-side-down, and fry 3 to 4 minutes on medium-high heat. Turn and fry the other side of each piece for an additional 3 to 4 minutes until golden brown.
9. Do not overcrowd the pieces during frying.
10. Place the fried chicken in the prepared baking pan in a single layer, skin-side-up.
11. Cover three sides of the pan, leaving one side uncovered for steam to escape.
12. Bake at 350°F for 35-40 minutes, then remove the foil and check for tenderness. Allow the chicken to bake uncovered for an additional 5 minutes to crisp the coating.
13. Serves 4. Leftovers can be refrigerated up to 4 days but should not be frozen. The leftover coating mix (first nine ingredients) can be stored at room temperature in a covered container for up to 2 months.
